channel.title: usdpr. — Dental reactivation field notes
channel.description: Honest, data-informed writing on dental patient reactivation, HIPAA-compliant SMS, PMS exports, pricing compliance, and the economics of bringing patients back.

item.title: The 5 segments of lapsed dental patients — usdpr.
item.description: A1, A2, B1, B2, B3. Five patient cohorts with wildly different reply rates. Write one generic message and you leave 40–60% on the table.

item.title: Broken appointment follow-up — the field guide for US dental practices
item.description: The practical playbook for recovering the patients who didn't show. 0–60 day, 60–180 day, and 180+ day windows, with the copy, the cadence, and the front-desk S

item.title: Dental hygiene recall statistics — 2026 benchmarks by practice size and specialty · usdpr.
item.description: Hygiene recall effectiveness varies 5× between the top and bottom decile of US practices. The 2026 benchmarks across 9 dimensions.

item.title: Dental practice patient attrition formula — the 3 versions and which to use · usdpr.
item.description: Three common formulas — 'lost patients,' 'lapsed,' and 'dormant' — each compute a different number. Pick wrong and your dashboard lies to you.

item.title: Dental practice transition — how to retain patients through ownership, location, or staff change · usdpr.
item.description: 20-35% of patients leave during a practice transition by default. With the playbook below, that number drops to 5-10%.

item.title: Dental reactivation ROI methodology — how we calculate your $77k loss number
item.description: Every number in our <a href='/calculator.html'>ROI calculator</a>, sourced and defended. If you're going to show your spouse or your CPA, use this.

item.title: Dental reactivation vs. new-patient acquisition — the ROI comparison every practice owner should see · usdpr.
item.description: Reactivating a lapsed patient costs 8-12× less than acquiring a new one. Data across 200+ practices showing why reactivation should be 80% of your marketing spe

item.title: Dental recall automation software — 2026 review of the 8 tools dentists actually use · usdpr.
item.description: Honest review of Weave, Solutionreach, RevenueWell, NexHealth, Dental Intel, Modento, Lighthouse 360, and usdpr. Bias disclosed upfront.

item.title: Dental recall call script — 4 that actually book patients on the first call · usdpr.
item.description: Phone scripts for recall calls that end with a confirmed appointment, not a voicemail. Each with the compliance guardrails office managers miss.

item.title: Dental recall text message examples — 7 that actually book patients
item.description: Copy-paste SMS templates for overdue hygiene, broken appointments, and long-lapsed patients. Every one TCPA-compliant, HIPAA-safe, and written by a real reactiv

item.title: Dentist patient retention playbook — 11 proven moves, ranked by impact · usdpr.
item.description: Retention moves ranked by statistical impact on year-over-year retention. The top 3 alone move retention +8-15 percentage points.

item.title: Dentrix broken appointment report — how to pull it, filter it, and actually use it · usdpr.
item.description: Step-by-step guide to extracting the list that actually matters. Most Dentrix users pull the wrong report.

item.title: Flat-fee vs. per-patient pricing for dental reactivation — usdpr.
item.description: Why pay-per-rebook pricing is illegal in Florida and risky in most states. The anti-kickback statutes every dental buyer should know.

item.title: HIPAA compliance for patient reactivation — usdpr.
item.description: The checklist your attorney will ask about: BAA provisions, PHI-in-body rules, audit logs, consent tracking, breach-notification clocks. Pasteable.

item.title: HIPAA SMS rules for dental practices — what you can and can't text patients
item.description: Exactly what HIPAA lets you include in a patient SMS (and what it doesn't). Also covers the TCPA 10DLC overlay, the STOP requirement, and where dental practices

item.title: How to reactivate lapsed dental patients — the 5-touch method that actually works
item.description: The field-tested sequence that takes your 1–3% single-send reactivation rate up to 8–15%. Written by the operator of a dental reactivation SaaS — no fluff, no f

item.title: Hygiene recall email wording — 9 openers that outperform 'time for your cleaning' · usdpr.
item.description: Subject lines + opening paragraphs that beat the default hygiene recall template. Data from 200+ dental practices' email sends.

item.title: Insurance-change dental patient retention — the 4-step re-engagement for ghosted patients · usdpr.
item.description: The #1 silent cause of dental patient churn: insurance change. Most practices lose these patients because they never knew the insurance was the issue.

item.title: No-show appointment fee policies that actually reduce no-shows (without angering patients) · usdpr.
item.description: A $50 no-show fee posted on a sign doesn't reduce no-shows — it reduces patients. The 4 policies below reduce no-shows 40-60% AND improve retention.

item.title: Open Dental CSV export for reactivation campaigns — field map + gotchas · usdpr.
item.description: Open Dental has the cleanest patient-data export of any major PMS. Here's exactly which fields you need, which to skip, and the 3 gotchas that break reactivatio

item.title: Overdue dental patient letter templates — 5 mailers that beat the generic postcard · usdpr.
item.description: The 'we miss you' postcard converts at 0.5-1%. These 5 letter templates convert at 6-12% because they acknowledge a specific reason the patient didn't come back

item.title: How a 5,000-patient practice recovers $50k in 90 days — usdpr.
item.description: The math behind dental patient reactivation: lapsed ratios, reply rates, book rates, and attach-rate upside. Real numbers, no hype.

item.title: Dental practice marketing budget — the 2026 allocation that actually produces ROI · usdpr.
item.description: Most practices allocate 60-70% of marketing spend to new-patient channels. The data says 30-40% is optimal. Here's the breakdown.

item.title: SMS vs email vs phone for dental recall — which channel wins (spoiler: it's the wrong question) · usdpr.
item.description: Single-channel comparisons systematically underestimate the compounding effect. Here's the real data.

item.title: Weave vs. NexHealth vs. RevenueWell vs. Solutionreach — honest 2026 comparison — usdpr.
item.description: Four dental-SaaS incumbents. Where each wins for reactivation. Where each loses. The honest buyer

item.title: Weave vs Solutionreach vs RevenueWell — 2026 dental reactivation comparison
item.description: Honest head-to-head comparison of the three big dental-comms suites on the one feature that drives the most revenue — patient reactivation. Plus where a dedicat
